original_abstract:
The crystal structure of Cd3As2—a high-mobility semimetal known for hosting electronic states that mimic Dirac particles—is reinvestigated using single-crystal X-ray diffraction. Contrary to previous reports of a noncentrosymmetric arrangement, our findings reveal that the material is actually centrosymmetric. Cd3As2 adopts a distorted antifluorite (M2X) superstructure, characterized by a tetragonal unit cell with lattice parameters a = 12.633(3) Å and c = 25.427(7) Å, crystallizing in the I41/acd space group. This superstructure is composed of distorted Cd6□2 cubes (where □ denotes an empty vertex) arranged in parallel columns and stacked with alternating chirality. 

Electronic structure calculations based on this centrosymmetric model yield results comparable to prior noncentrosymmetric simulations, but with a critical distinction: the presence of inversion symmetry identifies Cd3As2 as a true three-dimensional (3D) Dirac semimetal. In this configuration, all bands are spin-degenerate, and the material features a four-fold degenerate bulk Dirac point at the Fermi level along the Γ-Z path in the Brillouin zone. Consequently, Cd3As2 serves as a robust 3D electronic analog of graphene. Furthermore, scanning tunneling microscopy reveals a 2 × 2 surface reconstruction on the (112) cleavage plane, while morphological analysis shows that needle-shaped crystals preferentially grow along the [110] axis.
humanized_abstract:
The crystal structure of the well-known high mobility topological semimetal Cd3As2 is reexamined using single-crystal X-ray diffraction and was found to be centrosymmetric. Cd3As2 has a distorted antifluorite (M2X) (Cd3As2) superstructure with a tetragonal unit cell of dimensions a = 12.633(3) Å and c = 25.427(7) Å, belonging to the I41/acd space group. More specifically, the crystal structure of Cd3As2 comprises of distorted Cd6□2 cubes (□ denotes an empty vertex at the cube’s vertex), presented in the form of parallel columns and alternating stacks with chiralities along the c axis.

Confining the calculation to the centrosymmetric space group, P 6 3 22, with a structure almost identical to the noncentrosymmetric case, the electronic structure calculations yield similar predictions for the electronic transport properties, but with one important difference. The inversion centre implies that Cd3As2 is a three-dimensional Dirac semimetal. The spin-degeneracy of all bands, as well as a four-fold degenerate bulk Dirac point at the Fermi level along the Γ-Z direction in the Brillouin zone implies that Cd3As2 is an electronic 3D graphene. Scanning tunnelling microscopy measurements on the (112) cleavage plane reveal a 2 × 2 surface reconstruction. The needle-like morphology of the crystals indicates preferential growth along the [110] direction.

More specifically, the crystal structure of Cd3As2 comprises of distorted Cd6□2 cubes (□ denotes an empty vertex at the cube’s vertex), presented in the form of parallel columns and alternating stacks with chiralities along the c axis.

Confining the calculation to the centrosymmetric space group, P 6 3 22, with a structure almost identical to the noncentrosymmetric case, the electronic structure calculations yield similar predictions for the electronic transport properties, but with one important difference.
